fromRUSSELL 	thisISSUE
For the past 10+ years I’ve been studying two very important things, offer creation
and traffic generation. But over the past few months I’ve watched as a few companies
created offers that were so viral, the offer also became the traffic strategy.
To illustrate what I’m talking about, I want to share with you the story of Naama Bloom.
She started a subscription based website sending her customers tampons in the mail.
At first the company was very small, and she was actually going to Costco, buying the
tampons in bulk, and then shipping them out to her new customers. Naama decided to
make a video to explain what her service did. She knew some people who were trying
to break into the film and TV business and volunteered to do the video for free. They
brainstormed some funny ideas, and came up with the concept behind this video:
DotComSecrets Labs | 1
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

2 | September 2013

or reproduced in any way without the prior written permission of

The information services and resources provided in this book
are based upon the current internet marketing environment. The
techniques presented have been extraordinarily lucrative and
rewarding. Because the internet is constantly changing, the sites
and services presented in this book may change, cease or expand
with time. We hope that the skills and knowledge acquired from
this course will provide you with the ability to adapt to inevitable
internet evolution. However, we cannot be held responsible for
changes that may affect the applicability of these techniques.

based on our own testing and are only estimates of what we
think you could earn. There is no assurance you will do as
well as stated in any examples. If you rely upon any figures
provided, you must accept the entire risk of not doing as well
as the information provided.

In just a few days HelloFlo.com went from very few customers, to a video that has been
viewed over six million times, and overnight grew this company from obscurity into a very
large business. This same thing happened last year with the launch of DollarShaveClub.com
whose video has now been seen over 11 million times, and they have over 200,000
active
subscribers! Another great video you can see at PooPourri.com has created over four million
customers helping this company make somewhere between $60 to $80
million.
I’ve been watching these companies explode and go viral with no out of pocket advertising
money and I realize that
this is one of the most pure forms of word of mouth advertising.
Even without purchasing the product, people are passing the message to all of their
friends, fans and followers.
So, my question for you, is how can
you use this concept in your business? We’re going to
test it out on some of our physical products and see if we can create a video that will catch
on. We may attempt it for some of our information products as well, but we are definitely
going to focus a lot more on creating content, videos and more that others will want to
pass along to their friends - because when your offer also generates millions of visitors for
you... that’s power.
Russell Brunson, the “Overnight Success Maker”
Scan the QR code or view the
Hello Flo Video by going to the
URL below:
http://www.HelloFlo.com
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 2 of 48 --

fromRUSSELL
OF AN
By Russell Brunson, the Overnight Success Maker
I'm not sure about you, but my favorite part of this business
is creating new offers. That's probably why we've created
hundreds of offers in over a dozen niches (and my team
wants to kill me because I don't "FOCUS" enough). It's one
of the things I enjoy the most and keeps me excited to wake
up each day.
But, what happens when an offer you create doesn't work...?
I'm hoping that I'm not the only one who spends months
creating an offer and a sequence, and then not much
happens after launching it for some reason.
We always hear about the winners... but what about the
losers? Well, that's what I want to talk about today. Because
when you understand the power of direct response
marketing, you can simply
look, and the numbers will show
DotComSecrets Labs | 3
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 3 of 48 --

4 | September 2013
you what's broken. So, about a year ago, we started a new
weight loss offer called "Body Evolution."
We spent a lot of money on the domain name and the
trademark, and then started creating the offer.
We looked at what others were doing in the market so we
could model the successful offers, but we wanted to find our
own angle. After seeing hundreds of offers, we decided to
make a weight loss offer that didn't talk much about what
you can or can't eat, but to focus on what I feel is the only real
thing that matters in losing weight... your mind.
We put together a really good product teaching Neuro-
Linguistic Programming (NLP) principles that anyone can
use to help reach their goals. We then hired someone who
I think is the best living copywriter on earth, and had him
write the sales letter for the front end product, as well as
three upsells.
The letter turned out awesome, so we turned it into a sales
video and hired graphic designers and programmers to create
the funnel. After months of work and tens of thousands of
dollars spent, our masterpiece was ready to launch.
Now, before we contacted any affiliates, we wanted to
see if the offer would convert, so we purchased a solo ad
and started driving traffic. Within about 48 hours we knew
something was wrong. We had spend $3,000 on the ad, and
only made back about $700. Now, I'm no mathematician,
but those numbers aren't very good.
If you read last month’s issue, you know that the
one saving
thing from our $3,000 investment that made it worthwhile
was our test of these two subject lines:
• 	WARNING: they are trying to keep you fat :(
• 	my weight loss "dirty" little secret...
We found that the subject line “my weight loss "dirty"
little secret...” destroyed the other headline. The first
big
problem I saw was that our sales video was based around
the concept of the headline that lost. I was hoping that
it would be an easy fix, so we just changed the headlines
on the squeeze and sales pages to match the winning
subject line. I then went out, paid for a $5,000 ad and
crossed my fingers.
After two or three days we started to look closely at the
stats. While our engagement was a lot better on the
squeeze pages and sales page, the funnel still wasn't even
getting close to break even. In fact our $5,000 ad brought in
under $2,000. Once again, not good.
Now, at this point I was about ready to throw in the towel. I
was frustrated, and I felt like we were throwing money out
the door. What were we doing wrong?
And that's when we decided to step back, and
stop
looking at the conversions of just one or two pages, but
look at the conversions of the
whole funnel... And what we
saw gave us some
very interesting insights.
Let me show you what we found:
1st - Squeeze Page
As you can see in the screenshot above, we modeled the
same squeeze page that we have been testing on our other
offers, and just like a champ, it worked again! (YEAH! That's
the power of modeling success folks - stop trying to re-
invent the wheel). We averaged a 38% conversion on this
page which I am very proud of.
2nd - The Sales Video Page
We learned some interesting things on this page. First, we
had tested a man version of the video and a woman version.
Each had the same offer, but because we have a couple as
the guru's for this site, we decided to have both options.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 4 of 48 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 5
fromRUSSELL
...We decided to step back, and stop looking at the
conversions of just one or two pages, but look at the
conversions of the whole funnel... And what we saw
gave us some
very interesting insights.”
Squeeze Page	
Sales Page
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 5 of 48 --

6 | September 2013
We found that the male version of the video converted a lot
better than the female version.
I thought this was interesting, so I contacted some of my
friends in the weight loss niche, and they all said that for
some reason videos coming from a female voice just don't
convert as well as videos with a male voice.
So, while I was trying to outsmart them by having two
versions it turns out they had already tested it and knew the
masculine voice converted better.
Next we tested four versions of the video:
• 	Cartoon Animation
• 	Hand Sketched
• 	Powerpoint
• 	Powerpoint With Music
Now, this test is a little hard, because I hired someone who
was very cheap to do the hand sketched video. While it turned
Upsell 1
Results With Male’s Voice
Results With Female’s Voice
We found that the male
version converted a lot better
than the female version.”
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 6 of 48 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 7
fromRUSSELL
out good, it definitely wasn't great like other animations we
have done. So don't use this test to say that hand animated
videos don't work - in most of the tests I've seen, they have
out-converted powerpoints... What I am saying is start with a
powerpoint video, get it converting awesome, and
then hire an
artist to try to beat the control.
So, on the female version of the video, the powerpoint
without
any music track converted the highest... And the male version
with the music track beat out the other controls.
(NOTE: I've wanted to know for a while if music on
powerpoints would do better or worse but this test didn't
help much... ha ha... I'll do more testing on this in the future,
because I'm
hoping music helps. I can't stand PPT videos
without music).
3rd - The Upsells
Now, I've got to be honest, this was where I had stopped
looking at the stats for the first two months we were running
this offer. And truth be told, I was about ready to walk away
and give up on it until I asked Todd to pull the conversion
Upsell 2	
Upsell 3
25%CONVERSION RATE
FOR UPSELL #1 - OUR
TOP PERFORMING UPSELL
19%CONVERSION RATE
FOR UPSELL #2
16%CONVERSION RATE
FOR UPSELL #3
WE WERE HAPPY WITH THE RESULTS
OF OUR UPSELLS - IN FACT IT MADE US
TAKE ANOTHER LOOK AT AN OFFER WE
WERE ABOUT TO ABANDON.
HERE ARE THE NUMBERS SHOWING
HOW EACH UPSELL DID:
OUR THREE
1-TIME OFFERS
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 7 of 48 --

8 | September 2013
stats on the upsells. Check out what he found:
• 	Upsell 1 converted at a WHOPPING 25%. All I can say
about that is...
wow.
• 	Upsell 2 converted at 19% - also pretty awesome!!!
• 	Upsell 3 was a $1 trial, and I thought it would have
done a little better, but it didn't do too bad at 16%
conversion!
Hmmm... so isn't that interesting?
The Problem
I always tell people that the power of direct response
marketing is we can look at each step of the process, and
see what is broken, then just focus on the problem.
So, let me show you the overall stats of this funnel, and
then let me know if you can see what's broken, and what I
need to fix?
• 	Squeeze page => 38% conversion
• 	Sales Page => 0.76% conversion
• 	OTO #1 => 25% conversion
• 	OTO #2 => 19% conversion
• 	OTO #3 => 16% conversion
Yup... the problem is that pesky offer. It turns out that
no one really wants to use NLP to lose weight. I really
thought that concept would be hot, and get people
excited, but apparently not. Yet everything else in the
funnel is performing awesome.
Do you see the power of stepping back and looking at
how your entire funnel is performing?
Moving forward this month, we have a few options we are
going to test. First, we
know that the headline "Dirty Little
Secret" converts, so we need to make the new offer related
to that. Second, we know OTO 1 converts great - so maybe
we will turn that into the front end offer...
Sometimes this business feels like we're searching for
buried treasure, but the reality is, that’s exactly what
we're doing. I know that as soon as we can get the front
end offer to three percent conversions, we have an offer
that will make us over $1 million a year. So isn't it worth
fighting for?
So let me ask you - what changes do you need to make to
your offer now? DCS
Daily marketing tips you can
implement on your commute to the
office. Listen FREE by subscribing at:
www.MarketingInYourCar.com
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

10 | September 2013
HELLO! WANT TO GIVE US SOME MONEY?
Control	
With Hello Bar
Test by Russell Brunson and Todd Dickerson
I'm sure that most of you have websites that get traffic,
but you never really monetize it that well. That is how my
main blog has been for years. We get thousands of visitors
everyday and get a percentage to optin, but the rest just
come, learn some stuff and then leave.
Then one day Todd decided to throw up a "HELLO" bar at
the top pointing people back to our DCS Local webinar.
We ended up getting a click through rate of 3.45% on the
HELLO bar, 2.76% of the people who clicked registered for
the webinar, and from those leads we are averaging about
$10 DPR (Dollars Per Registrant). Yes, that means that we are
making an
extra $276 for every 1,000 visitors who come to
our blog just by adding in this HELLO bar!
And the best part is that it also caused a 6.13% lift in regular
optins (13.23% vs. 14.04%)! So it didn't decrease our regular
engagement at all!
3.45%OF BLOG VISITORS CLICKED
ON THE HELLO BAR
2.76%OF THOSE VISITORS REGISTERED
FOR OUR DCS LOCAL WEBINAR
$276MORE MONEY WE EARNED
FOR EVERY 1,000 VISITORS TO
OUR BLOG AFTER ADDING THE
“HELLO” BAR
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

DotComSecrets Labs | 11
Written by Russell Brunson
Okay, so I have the type of personality where I get excited
really often... but seeing the results of this test literally kept
me up all night. (Yes, I really am that big of a marketing
nerd). We've been testing all sorts of awesome stuff to
increase conversions on our video sales pages, and this
idea had never even crossed my mind.
Check out what Kimberly Snyder did on her VSL page above.
Yes, her "Add To Cart" button is
above the video! Not only
is that a cool test, but it increased her conversions by a
whopping 38%! Her thought was that many people get so
engaged in your video that they never even scroll down and
see the "Add To Cart" button. This is one I'm going to test
out tonight and I'll let you know our results next month!
ADD TO CART WHERE!?!?
38%INCREASE WHEN THE “ADD TO
CART” BUTTON WAS MOVED
ABOVE THE SALES VIDEO
CONVERSION RATE
“Add to Cart” Button Above Video
FREE VIDEO TRAINING
Test by James Francis
The most widely accepted listbuilding tactic is to show your
main offer immediately after somebody submits their email
address on your squeeze page. This allows as many people
as possible to see your offer without fail, leading to a higher
amount of sales. But as everyone is doing this nowadays,
the question is... is it still as effective as it used to be? I tested
this out to find the answer for myself.
Version one of the squeeze page sent people straight to
the sales video as normal while version two sent people
to a “pre-launch style” video tutorial series with an orange
“Yes! Let Me In!” button below each free video tutorial.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 11 of 48 --

12 | September 2013
Everything else was the same.
So which made the most sales? Although this goes against
pretty much every sales funnel tutorial out there, giving
free valuable content to your audience before showing
them the offer apparently makes more sales.
The logic in this is pretty obvious when you think about
it, as the free videos create trust and authority status,
“warming” the lead to pay more attention to your offer.
Plus it shows you actually care about your audience’s
results, because you’re delivering value before asking for
anything in return. So it’s a win/win all around!
Obviously every sales funnel is different, so I’d recommend
trying this out for yourself – but this result has been the
same across all my clients’ sales funnels too.
To implement this into your own sales funnel, just create
three to five video tutorials around your main offer’s topic,
then drip feed them to your new leads via a follow-up
sequence. Then change your thank you page URL to the first
free video tutorial, and you’re good to go.
.2% 	.31%Control 	Free Video Series
59%increase
Control 	Free Video Series
FREE VIDEO TRAINING, CONT.
CONVERSION RATE
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 12 of 48 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 13
BUY NOW VS. FREE TRIAL
By Russell Brunson
I recently read a cool article from Neil Patel from
QuickSprout.com, where he was showing the conversion
that GetResponse.com got on one of their recent tests. Here
is what Neil posted:
You already know free trials convert better than a single option
forcing people to buy now. But do you know by how much?
GetResponse used to have just a Buy Now button on their
homepage. But when they added a Free Trial button to their
homepage, their signup rate went up by 158.60%.
Granted, their revenue didn’t go up by 158.60% because
some users cancel during the free trial period, but the
overall revenue increase should still be well into the double
digit percentages.
If you haven’t tried leveraging a free trial strategy, you
should consider testing it as I’ve never seen it lose...
assuming you are offering a good product or service.
Buy Now Button 	Buy Now + Free Trial Button
158.6%MORE SIGNUPS
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 13 of 48 --

14 | September 2013
Test by Russell Brunson and Todd Dickerson
Over the past few months we have been testing showing
the order button immediately on our video sales letters
(VSLs) instead of hiding them until we reveal the price. In
almost all of our tests (in the niches we are in) hiding the
order button has hurt conversions.
We also found that having the order form on the same
page as the VSL (instead of opening into a new page) also
increased conversions. So, the next logical test was what
would happen if you just removed the order button all
together and immediately opened the order form. Seems
like a logical test, right?
Well, this one was a shocking disappointment. We had a
43.42% increase by hiding the order form, and a 66.04%
increase in EPC's.
What we learned is that people want the ability to order
earlier (so show them the button now), but they still like
to have the choice to click the "Add To Cart" button before
they commit to buy.
WHOA! NOT THAT FAST...
Order Form Shown Immediately 	Order Button With Hidden Form
$.45 	$.75Order Form Shown
Immediately Order Button With
Hidden Form
66%increase
EARNINGS PER CLICK
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 14 of 48 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 15
Test by Russell Brunson and Todd Dickerson
This is a question that I get asked a
lot, and I'm never
really sure what to say. There are two schools of thought.
• 	First, charge low prices so you can get a
lot of
customers.
• 	Second, charge high prices so you have less
customers and the same profits.
I'm not sure which is the right approach. Actually, I
normally do a combination of the two... Charge low on the
front end, and then super high on the backend.
We had been selling our pornography addiction product
for $47 even though we thought that it could and should
be sold at $97.
We ran a pricing test for a little while, but killed it fast
when we found out that we had
four times more sales at
$47 than we did at $97. So, for this product, we actually
got more customers
and more profits by charging less.
So, never assume you are right in pricing. Try testing out a
few options and see which ones win!
HOW MUCH SHOULD I CHARGE?
Lower $47 Price Point
.83% 	5.97%$97 Price Point 	$47 Price Point
616.4%increase
CONVERSION RATE
$97 PRICE1 SALE x $97 = $97
$47 PRICE4 SALES x $47 = $188 AND
4x AS MANY CUSTOMERS
EVEN THOUGH THE $47 OFFER CUT THE PRICE OF OVERCOME PORNOGRAPHY
IN HALF, WE ALMOST DOUBLED THE AMOUNT OF MONEY MADE.
LOWER PRICE
MORE MONEY
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 15 of 48 --

16 | September 2013
Test by Russell Brunson and Todd Dickerson
Last month we launched a new system called
20MinutePayday.com. This is a free system that helps
people promote three different business opportunities. I
could write a book on the strategy behind the site, but in
the first thirty days we received hundreds of thousands of
visitors and over 41,000 members.
We knew that a big surge of traffic was coming, so we were
excited to test several elements.
I want to note that we did have a version of this page
without the sales video on top, and it converted a lot higher,
41,000 OPTINS AND HOLDING STRONGStep 1	
Step 2
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 16 of 48 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 17
but our backend numbers getting people to upgrade
dropped dramatically, so we kept the video version because
it presold our members and more of them purchased after
they joined.
On this page we wanted to test several things that we’ve
found to be true in past testing:
• 	First, we added Facebook style testimonials at the
bottom of the page.
• 	Second, we added a video spoiler box under the
video.
• 	Third, we used the mini survey style squeeze page.
• 	Fourth, we asked for phone numbers in the account
creation process (something I thought was going to
hurt conversions).
• 	Fifth, we added a sales letter version of the offer as an
exit pop.
When all was said and done, and over 41,000 people had
joined, we found that the winning variation utilized all of
the testing tricks that we have been showing you in other
issues (as well as asking for the phone number). When
using all of these elements together we saw a 17% increase
in total conversion.
Control (Stripped Down Version)
Exit Pop
17%OVERALL LIFT IN
CONVERSIONS AFTER ALL
ELEMENTS WERE ADDED
TO THE CONTROL
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 17 of 48 --

18 | September 2013
Test by Russell Brunson and Todd Dickerson
We attempted to optimize our landing page for mobile
to improve conversion. Every expert is shouting from the
rooftops that you should make your landing pages 'mobile
responsive.' Maybe so... but not all the time.
If you're going to build a mobile responsive version think
it out as well as you think out your desktop version,
there are plenty of html/css frameworks that turn regular
designs into mobile sized ones with the resize of a
browser but often everything changes regarding your
presentation, the fold changes, the headline display
changes, the opt-in boxes change.
Test everything. Don't throw up a responsive version unless
you've tested and proven it out converts your regular one
with mobile viewers. We're not giving up on using mobile
responsive landing pages, we're just going back to the
drawing board and trying again. Just keep in mind if your
page already is usable on a mobile device, you may not
have much to gain from a fully mobile version. Stay tuned!
DON’T BLINDLY FOLLOW.
TEST EVERYTHINGControl	
Mobile Responsive Version
+7%INCREASE IN OPTINS
WITH MOBILE
RESPONSIVE SITE
-83%CHANGE IN PURCHASES
WITH MOBILE
RESPONSIVE SITE
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 18 of 48 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 19
Test by James Francis
Something I’ve always wanted to test is just how much
“stacking the proof” affects conversions, but only lately
have we had enough real success stories caught on video
to test this out.
Firstly, I emailed customers of our Six Figure Shortcut offer to
get their opinions about the course, asking for a short video
testimonial just out of good will. A few weeks later, we got a
huge amount of video testimonials back, which I then added
below the sales video.
You can see what the section below the sales video looked
like in the screenshot above... Then we just embedded all the
video testimonials we had all the way down the page, ending
with another “Add To Shopping Cart” button.
So which made the most sales? As you can see, the clear
winner which doubled our conversion rate was the
version with the video testimonials below the tutorial.
In my experience, there are two reasons why this
happened...
First, people are usually pretty cynical in the “make
money online” industry, so they’re always looking for an
unquestionable amount of proof. Having the headline of
“Need More Proof?” enters the conversation they’re already
having in their mind, then the videos instantly overcome the
“is this a scam?” objection.
Secondly, the people in the videos themselves are a vast
mix from all over the world. Customers will find at least
one person in the videos they can relate to, which makes
their buying decision a lot easier. Oh, and not to mention
the videos are an element of social proof too (i.e. “Hey, all
these people bought the product – I should too!”).
So whatever type of offer you have, I’d strongly recommend
collecting real video testimonials and adding them below
your sales video. It’ll make your offer look much more
credible, plus based on our results, it’ll increase your sales too.
STACKING THE PROOF
Testimonials Stacked Below Video
1.42% 	2.86%No Testimonials
Below Video Testimonials
Below Video
101%increase
CONVERSION RATE
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 19 of 48 --

20 | September 2013
A BIG RISK WITH A PAYOFF
Written by Roland Mirabueno, MindValleyInsights.com
One-Time-Offer or OTO pages happen to generate
great revenue for Mindvalley. An OTO page is the sales
page that visitors see right after they’ve signed up and
entered their email address, where we offer products at
an appealing discount for a limited period. This has been
a successful strategy so far, which is why we go to great
measures to innovate on these pages by applying careful
and extensive research.
Previously, Justyna Jastrzebska – Senior Partner at
Mindvalley, highlighted the importance of adding an intro
video in your funnel sequence for better engagement with
customers.
But we decided to take this concept a step further by testing
two OTO pages for our product Chakra Healing, using two
different frameworks:
• 	A Normal OTO that contains long copy similar to most
of our OTO pages and only reveals the intro video in the
funnel, and;
• 	A New OTO that features the pre-lesson intro video and
Long Copy Version (Control)	
Video Version
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 20 of 48 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 21
a timed order menu. This means that the menu is set
to appear during a specific time after the video starts
playing. So instead of including the video in the funnel,
we featured it on the OTO page, and the order menu
appears a few minutes after the visitor presses play.
Both require the customer to take a Chakra Quiz on the
landing page and submit their name and email address to
access the quiz results.
Here is the framework for the Normal OTO, with the pre-
lesson intro video featured in the Funnel:
• 	Take up the Chakra quiz on the landing page,
• 	Submit name and email to receive quiz results,
• 	Redirect to the OTO with long copy to purchase
product, and
• 	Welcome email on the funnel linking to lesson 0
And here is the New OTO framework, with the pre-lesson
included on the OTO page:
• 	Take up the Chakra quiz on the landing page,
• 	Submit name and email to receive quiz results,
• 	Redirect to the OTO page with lesson 0, and
• 	Welcome email on the funnel
This New OTO was a massive innovation and we had to
approach this test with a lot of caution, as we could’ve
ended up doing more harm than good if the test failed.
Here are some precautionary tips that we followed to
increase the probability of a successful test:
1. 	To engage people who signed up, we discussed the
Chakra Quiz Test on the video.
2. 	As mentioned, we timed the order menu. This way,
the customer wouldn’t be distracted by it before they
even begin watching the video.
3. 	We removed the long copy to keep the customer’s
attention on the video.
So how do you think the New OTO framework fared with
our sales? Did it hurt us or help us?
The answer is...
The new framework
boosted our sales on the OTO by 33%.
So the risk of innovating on a cash cow paid off.
The bottom line is, a risk is worth taking if you invest
enough research and time on innovating it. You will have to
deal with the polar opposites of the results, of course. If you
succeed, the increase in sales will be significant. And if you
fail, the decrease in sales will also be significant.
In our case, the 33% boost in sales benefited us immensely
because it became one our top-performing pages.
But it gave us a new perspective on how to plan future sales
campaigns and product launches. And ultimately, we were
able to understand our customers and prospects better.
33%INCREASE IN SALES WITH
VIDEO VERSION OVER
LONG COPY PAGE
EVEN THOUGH THEY WERE USED TO USING LONG COPY FOR THIS TYPE OF
OFFER, MINDVALLEY’S VIDEO OTO TEST PAYED OFF BIG TIME...
THE RISK AND
THE PAYOFF
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 21 of 48 --

22 | September 2013
230% BOOST IN CONVERSIONS
WITH A HEADLINE REDESIGN
Written by Natasha Zolotareva, MindValleyInsights.com
Most product pages pair up their headlines with an image,
but experience tells us that design can support as well as
hurt conversions. So we decided to test if changing our
headline deco could impact conversions. Check out these
two tests and their results, and feel free to emulate them
for your business:
1. Removing the Image
At the top right you can see the original headline design
on one of our landing pages for the Silva Life System. This
particular style added a touch of elegance to the site. But
we were concerned that the human faces might affect our
conversions.
As a rule of thumb, never put a human face next to a
headline. Human faces capture the visitor’s gaze instantly
and take attention away from the headline. So we
removed the image completely, including the background
color, to make the headline stand out. The result? A 7%
boost in opt-ins and up to 20% when we tested this on our
other pages as well.
This test proved that images of human faces, particularly
those that look directly into the camera, drag a customer’s
attention away from the message in the headline and
subhead. But there’s a problem here. The raw headline on
its own looks ugly and doesn’t help the elegance of the
brand. So we launched a second test.
2. Improving the Headline Design
This particular test was inspired by sites such as Square
Up and The Lean Startup, which uses a transparent or
defocused image as a background to their copy. For this, we
split-tested two different designs. This time we scored
big:
Control	
No Image
Light Blue Image	
Dark Blue Image
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 22 of 48 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 23
• 	Light Blue Image: 230.41% Boost
• 	Dark Blue Image: 112.05% Boost
Pretty awesome, right? Give it a try. To recap, here are some
tips before you start:
• 	If the image doesn’t communicate value – do without it.
• 	If using an image of a model, choose one that faces the
headline and not the camera. The human gaze pulls
eyeballs away from your headline.
• 	Use easy-to-read fonts on a light background, or
experiment with transparent images.
The philosophy here is copy is king, and design is the sidekick.
Written by Roland Mirabueno, MindValleyInsights.com
As a kid, I had a technique I used whenever I needed to ask
my mom for money. This is how it usually went:
Me: “Mom, I need fifty bucks for a school project.”
Mom: “Why so expensive? What type of project is it for?”
Me: “I’m only kidding! I only need thirty bucks. It’s for a
science project.”
And I would get the money without further inquisition from
the budget ministry! I figured that every time I needed to ask
for money, I would only have to justify the amount. Of course,
as a 10-year old, I didn’t have the necessary skills to prepare
a presentation or a spreadsheet in order to explain my costs.
But every time I needed money, I started at a higher price (at
times an exaggerated one) to eventually get my way.
Recently, I was doing some research on Order Menus when
I stumbled upon a concept called “Primacy Effect,” which I
read about on Visual Website Optimizer.
It’s a concept that affects the behavior of consumers by how
“multiple items are arranged in a list or catalog.” Basically,
how you arrange the packages of your product do have an
effect on what they buy or whether they buy at all.
Mindvalley products are usually offered in three packages:
Silver, Gold and Platinum. The Silver package is a digital
or downloadable product, the Gold a physical product
shipped to consumers and the Platinum collection is a
combination of the two.
Primacy Effect states that because people in general read
from left to right, and if the products are arranged in an
ascending order, customers will usually spot the cheapest
package first. But my childhood technique inversely applies
here too – if they see the cheapest package first, other
packages will automatically seem “expensive.”
For instance, you can see what our Order Menu on Silva Life
System looks like above. Customers would read the prices in
the order of $97, $197 and $297 (on an offer reduced to $197).
To apply the Primacy Effect concept, we decided to swap the
Silver and Platinum packages and test it against the original.
Can you guess if this test was helpful or hurtful to our sales?
By a difference of a whopping 71.92%... the new Order
Menu hurt our sales.
This seemed crazy, considering all the research that pointed
AVOID THIS WHEN DESIGNING
ORDER MENUS
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 23 of 48 --

24 | September 2013
to Primacy Effect as a driving force to boost sales. But it
didn’t work for us, and upon analyzing the case, here’s what
surfaced:
1. 	We only gave three options. Primacy Effect worked on
the basis that it draws the eyes from left to right, but
visually, it would only take a glance for a customer to
perceive our entire Order Menu. This cancels out the
desired effect due to the lack of ascend.
2. 	Technically, there were only two different prices. The
offer on the Platinum collection is perpetual. This was
designed to get people to buy the Platinum collection
because they are getting more for the price of the Gold
package. Because of the limited number of choices, it
was not as impactful.
3. 	Lastly, we put our premier choice at the left-most of the
Order Menu. We shouldn’t have done that. The choice
at the left-most usually offers the shock value – the
choice that makes customers go “Whoa this is pricey,”
and the premier option should’ve been somewhere
else. In our case, we hurt ourselves because our premier
option showcases the highest price among all.
Although our first experiment with Primary Effect didn’t
work in our favor, like all tests, it shed some good light on
certain features (in this case – our order menu) and enabled
us to read our visitors better.
Control	
Primacy Effect
-72%LESS SALES AFTER THE
“PRIMACY EFFECT” WAS
APPLIED TO THE ORDER
MENU
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 24 of 48 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 25
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 25 of 48 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 27
Test by Russell Brunson and Todd Dickerson
Last month we tested three different postcards and had
crowned a winner. But this month the reigning champ
went up against our new tearsheet. Now, in the past,
we have never had a postcard beat a tearsheet, but we
thought maybe this time it could happen.
So, we sent both to the mailing house, mailed each piece
to 1,500 people (for a total of 3,000 pieces sent) and waited
to see what would happen. After the results came back, we
had a definite winner!
Postcard: 20 trials
Tearsheet: 30 trials
So, you know what that means, right...? It's time for the roll
out! We're going to start buying a bigger hotline this month
with this tearsheet, and then start testing it with other
similar lists. Don't you
love marketing!
Neuracal Postcard (Previous Winner)
From Justin Brooke of IMScalable.com
I wanted to test this to see if ads including mobile converted
better than ads only shown on desktop, including newsfeed
and right hand column. We’re all on our phones all day
every day. But, how often do we click on one of the ads
in our newsfeed as we’re scrolling through Facebook and
actually opt-in to a page? Me, not so often.
Let’s take a look at the benefits of targeting All (including
Mobile) opposed to targeting only desktop. For starters, your
audience is going to be larger. This targeting includes mobile,
that makes sense.
As it turns out, our Click-Thru-Rate was actually higher by
almost 0.07%. But, as we all know (at least we should) CTR
isn’t always the best measurement of how good an ad is.
We saw a 5% higher conversion rate with the desktop only ad
vs. the ad targeting All and a lead cost of only $3.62 vs $6.11.
That’s a 60% cost difference!
MOBILE AND DESKTOP AD TARGETING
$6.11 	$3.62Targeting All 	Targeting Desktop
40%savings
COST PER LEAD
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 27 of 48 --

28 | September 2013
From Justin Brooke of IMScalable.com
The way Facebook makes money is by people clicking on
ads. Someone obviously has to be browsing Facebook in
order to click an ad, so the longer Facebook can keep a
person within their site, the better.
Understanding this, let’s look at how to drive traffic to your
landing page by appearing to send traffic to a Facebook
page, but actually sending them to your URL. You can
achieve this by using an iframe redirect. We use Woobox
and it is very simple to setup. Create a fangate tab and
select redirect, enter your tracking url, save setting, and
choose this tab for your ad to go to.
We’ve tested this over and over and our results have always
been much better than driving traffic to an external url.
For example, we switched from driving traffic to an external
url to using a fanpage tab and lead costs decreased by over
300%! In addition, your Fanpage will generate engagement.
What we learned: Don’t include mobile when setting
up your ads, unless the ad you’re running is a mobile
specific campaign. Also, you’ll want to setup newsfeed
ad campaigns separately from sidebar ad campaigns.
We’ll have some test results explaining more about this
next month.
Stop driving traffic away from Facebook! They don’t like it.
Use a Fanpage tab redirect to drive traffic to your landing
pages and watch your CTR go up and your CPC go down.
Do you need traffic? If you would like to have your
campaigns managed by a team of experts, check out our
case studies at IMScalable.com or contact us at info@
imscalable.com
KEEPING TRAFFIC ON
FACEBOOK, SORTA
Traffic to Fanpage Tab CPA = $2.87 	Traffic to External Link CPA = $10.63
$512.27 spent
/ 178 leads
= $2.87 CPA

30 | September 2013
AD DESIGN CHEAT SHEET, CONT.
Written by Russell Brunson and DC Fawcett
Last summer I had a chance to hang out with DC Fawcett,
and became super impressed with him as a marketer.
During our mastermind meeting he showed some of his
direct mail campaigns and I was so excited and impressed
I asked if he’d be willing to share some of his tests with the
DCS Labs members. Let me walk you through his postcard
tests and the funnels he drove his traffic to.
In this first test, he sent out three different postcards, each
to the same number of people to determine a winner.
Postcard two with an image of a personalized check won
giving him a 126% return on investment.
• 	Postcard 1 lost $195, had a CPL of $9 and an ROI of -81%
• 	Postcard 2 made a profit of $304, had a CPL of $7 and
an ROI of 126%
• 	Postcard 3 made a profit of $418 but the CPL was higher
than Postcard 2 at $8. The ROI for this postcard was 86%.
Here’s what DC said about these results:
THE PERSONALIZED CHECK
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 30 of 48 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 31
Postcard 1 (Front and Back)
“Postcard 2 was the winner of our test with the highest
return on investment. We are using this postcard for this
product offer now that we have determined a winner.
Always split test different copy before spending a lot of
money on a campaign because you want to determine the
copy that will give you the best bang for your buck.”

34 | September 2013
THE RIGHT PRICE
Written by Russell Brunson and DC Fawcett
I thought that this test was a really cool one. Its goal was to
figure out which price point would be the winner. Rarely
do I ever split test price points, so it got me excited to
start looking closer at what I’m charging so I can test some
other variations.
They ran this test using the winning postcard from The
Personalized Check test (page 30) with the following prices:
$37, $47, $77, $97, 2 payments of $37, 2 payments of $47, 2
payments of $97, 3 payments of $37, 3 payments of $47.
The three funnels shown were created in order to
implement this test.
As you can see from the results above, 3 x $37 won the test
with $16.15 EPC and $2,664 in total revenue.
Funnel 1
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 35 of 48 --

36 | September 2013
By Brett Curry, CEO of OnlineMarketingGiant.com
There was a day when almost anyone could win with paid
search. CPCs (cost-per-clicks) used to be about $.10 for
some keywords, and it didn’t take a whole lot of effort or
money to create wins. Fast forward to today and some
keywords will smack your wallet to the tune of $80 or $90
(or higher) per click. Even in non-competitive categories
you’ll likely need to shell out a few bucks per click.
Paid search can still be a gold mine, but it can also be a
minefield. Here’s some guidance to help you avoid the
traps and pitfalls that have claimed the PPC lives of many
would-be online marketers.
When done properly, paid search marketing can be a
huge driver of profitable traffic. For most of our clients we
position paid search as a must, not as an option. As with
all things search-related Google absolutely dominates in
the paid search world. Yes, you can buy ads through Bing,
and if you are selling nationally you could and probably
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 36 of 48 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 37
should consider it. However Google owns at least 67% of
the search market. Many in the industry (myself included)
believe that number is closer to 80%. So in this article I’ll
focus on winning with Adwords. Once you win there, then
you can expand to other paid search platforms. Here’s
how you can win with Adwords specifically, but many of
these will apply to any platform:
1. Get In Your Searcher’s Head
I talk a lot about this when speaking from the stage,
consulting with clients, or writing here. You have to
know your market. You also have to understand what
the intent is behind different search queries (search
phrases). When someone types in “new brown leather
shoes” and you sell brown leather shoes, what type of ad
should you run and what page on your site should you
send searchers to? Or, better yet, do you even want to
show an ad to someone who simply types in “new brown
leather shoes?” Or should you save your money and only
show ads for more specific searches like “new dockers
brown leather shoes?” Searches for brand names typically
imply a stronger buying intent. Brand or product specific
searches are usually made by someone who is closer to
making a purchase than someone who searches with a
more generic query.
2. Build Landing Pages That Google And
Your Visitors Will Love
Give your site visitors what they want immediately. Don’t
send visitors to your home page when you have a product
page or a specific landing page that would be more
appropriate based on their search. Your conversions will
To dominate with Adwords you really need
to win on two main fronts - Quality Score,
and Bidding. Let’s look quickly at each:
Quality Score
This is similar to Google's algorithm for
PageRank that they use to rank sites in the
organic search results. Your quality score
is based on a 10 point scale with 0 being
terrible and 10 being mind-blowingly
amazing. You are given a quality score for
each keyword you bid on, not for each ad.
So with the same ad you may have an 8/10
quality score for one keyword and a 4/10 for
another keyword. Google considers three
main factors when giving you a quality score:
• 	Expected click-through-rate. It makes
sense that Google would want to show
ads that people are likely to click on. That’s
how they make money. They are pretty
good at determining ahead of time if
someone will click on your ad or not.
• 	Landing page relevancy. Will your landing
page likely give searchers what they are
looking for? If so, Google’s happy. If not,
your quality score, and therefore, position
will suffer.
• 	Ad relevancy. Does your ad copy speak
directly to the need of the searcher and
does it include the keywords in it? If
these three are positive, then you will
have a decent or better quality score.
Bidding
Google will actually reward you with lower
CPC rates if you have a high quality score
and high click-through-rate, but you will
still have to bid competitively. Google will
tell you how much you need to bid to show
up on page one. Bid below that and guess
what? You won’t appear on page one.
DOMINATING GOOGLE
dcsLOCAL	www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 37 of 48 --

38 | September 2013
be much higher and Google will love you in the process.
3. Tightly Focused Ad Groups
Build your ad groups tightly around very similar
keywords. If you sell shoes online and you sell men’s
and women’s shoes of all types and styles, you wouldn’t
want just one ad group for all shoe-related search
queries. Narrow your focus and only put very related
keywords into each adgroup. This will raise your quality
score and CTR and in the end make it easier to spot
keywords you should spend more money on and areas
where you should spend less.
4. Straightforward But Bold Copy
You’ll want to use keywords in your copy, but you also
need to break through the clutter. Don’t get so cute
and creative that visitors don’t know what you are
selling. Remember that searchers scan, they don’t read
everything from top to bottom. They scan for what
catches their eye and then they read selectively.
An online marketing colleague in Canada worked with a
tattoo removal business and one of their best performing
ads read “Tattoo Removal Service: Its expensive, but not
as much as years of regret” or something very similar to
that. It was true, specific and drove a ton of clicks and
conversions. All of their competitors’ ads had Tattoo
Removal as well, but that interesting line helped in this
particular case.
While good ad writing rules apply here, there is no absolute
template to follow. You will have to test. You want the
keywords in the ad, that’s a given. You want the copy to
be benefit oriented pointing out selling points like large
inventory, same day delivery, free delivery, discounts,
exclusive lines, etc. You also want some kind of call to
action like a “free consultation.” But how you present those
to get the most clicks will really come down to testing.
5. Aggressively Manage And Grow Your
Negative Keyword List
A negative keyword is any word you
don’t want to run ads
for. So if you sell new shoes online, you would want to add
“used” as a negative keyword so you wouldn’t show up for
any searches for “used shoes” (which interestingly, there
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 38 of 48 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 39
dcsLOCAL
is a market for used shoes online). You can brainstorm a
pretty good list of negative keywords at the outset, but you
will need to refine this as you go. Overtime you’ll see some
searches pop up that surprise you. Consistently manage
and expand your negative keyword list.
6. Change Bids On The Keyword Level
Bidding strategy can get pretty complex and for really
large campaigns some automated options might be
in order. Regardless, you will need to drill down to the
keyword level for bidding. Some keywords will drive more
conversions than others and you won’t really know what
keywords will perform and what keywords won’t until
the campaign runs for a month or two. If one keyword
brings you a 2% conversion and another keyword brings
a 0% conversion you should adjust your bids and landing
pages accordingly. Maybe a keyword isn’t converting
because of a landing page issue - or maybe it’s just a bad
keyword to target.
7. Advanced Placement Setup
In some cases you will want to limit the time and even
days that your ads are shown. In almost all cases you will
at least want to limit the locations in which your ads are
displayed. We work with a client who is in the IT field and
they found that clicks that came in overnight (yes they did
get a decent amount) didn’t convert at all. Or if they did
get any response from clicks that came in overnight it was
for people looking for a job. Not ideal in the case. You may
find that searches from a particular state or area just don’t
convert for whatever reason. You can limit exposure in
those areas or eliminate your exposure there all together.
8. Position Matters
Showing up in the top three ad result positions will play
a big role in the overall success of the campaign. We
recently consulted with a client who had lost about 40%
of their revenue generated from adwords and, needless
to say, were in a bit of a panic. We found that the culprit
was some bad ad changes that moved them from an
average position of 2.4 to an average position of 4.6 for a
few big, money-making keywords. They lost over 60% of
their traffic for those keywords. We are working now to
get them back to their former glory.
Aside from driving profitable traffic there are a few other
reasons to consider paid search. One, it gives you great
data that you can’t always get elsewhere. If you check
your Google Analytics account you will see “not provided”
growing every month in your keyword report. For a lot of
sites over 50% of their organic traffic shows up in the not
provided area. This is problematic because we can’t see
what keywords the majority of our site visitors are typing
in to get to our site.
With adwords, you can see 100% of the keywords people
type in to get to your ads. This can fill in some important
holes in your website data. Also, it allows you to quickly
and somewhat inexpensively test keywords to see how
profitable they are before you invest a lot of time and
effort into SEO for those keywords.
Paid search is not a game for the faint of heart, but it
is one that you can succeed at. Every company serious
about driving leads online should consider it. DCS
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 39 of 48 --

40 | September 2013
FIGURES A YEAR
& GROWING
“THE TOP FOUR THINGS
WE’VE DONE TO GROW
OUR BUSINESS...”
By Chaunna and Justin Brooke of IMScalable.com
Thanks to Russell, in 2007 we were able to start up a
website that provided us a six-figure income. That was
great for many years... until we realized we were stuck
at six-figures. There seemed to be a ceiling we would
hit and almost bounce off of. This year we finally broke
through that ceiling.
We’ve gone from a husband and wife home business to
now having a team of seven and an actual office that we
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 40 of 48 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 41
work in with really, really big white boards. J The four
lessons below are how we broke our ceiling and we hope
it helps you grow too.
1. Build A Business Not A Website
About two years ago we were hitting a plateau in our
earnings. Month after month we’d hit the same five-figure
number but seemed to be at a glass ceiling. Now, I’m not
complaining about a five-figure monthly income, but that
income came with a hefty workload and we just couldn’t
outwork ourselves to reach the next level.
We knew we had a good product, we knew we had
expertise on our topic, we knew a lot, but it wasn’t
enough to help us increase our monthly income. There
had to be another factor we were missing... the elusive
“missing piece” if you will. That’s when we hired a
business coach.
On our first call with him, he asked what our business
plan was, what our quarterly strategic plan was, what our
budget looked like... He was speaking a language I didn’t
understand. I knew that we had a website that made
money - I knew that website provided a very comfortable
lifestyle, but we didn’t have a “business.” That was a real
slap in the face, but one we needed if we were ever going
to have that seven-figure dream business.
We made a plan to meet with him twice a week. I
meet with him on Tuesdays and Justin meets with
him on Wednesdays. Why separate? It’s actually super
beneficial... if working with your spouse isn’t hard
enough - try making a business decision with one
person on each side of the fence. Our business coach
can act as a third party because he knows my views and
Justin’s views and how the decision affects both our
positions in the company.
Over the first three months with our new business coach
we followed his system closely. We worked to create a
business development plan, we created a strategic plan,
and we even created a budget that was more defined
than “yes more money is coming in than going out.”
It was long and exhausting, but the effort was worth it. In
just the first three months, our revenue doubled. Finally...
we broke through that income ceiling.
2. Quarterly Attack Plan
The second most important change we made to grow our
business was implementing a Strategic Plan. This is like a
quarterly goals sheet but on steroids. At the end of each
quarter we ask ourselves these things:
• 	What went right?
• 	What were our challenges?
• 	What do we want to focus on in the next quarter?
• 	What are three specific outcomes we want in the next
90 days?
• 	How does that affect our budget?
Answering these questions are vital to growth each
quarter.
dcsSUCCESS	www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 41 of 48 --

42 | September 2013
We can identify our strengths, make notes of our
challenges and how we overcame them (or address
how to eliminate them in the future). We also set a goal
for three
big things we’ll do for the next quarter. The
last question ensures that our business can afford the
changes we want to make in the next quarter; sometimes
that’s hiring a new person, paying a freelancer for a
big
programming project - whatever it is, we make sure it fits
within the budget before moving forward.
Once our three goals are identified, we don’t change
direction when the next shiny opportunity comes along.
We
focus solely on what we discussed at the end of the
previous quarter. This has helped us eliminate that feeling
of overwhelm, not jump from shiny ball to shiny ball, and
not spend money on products we don’t need.
By focusing on the quarter we’re giving ourselves
enough time to see if an idea is successful - if it’s not,
we can pivot directions next quarter - if it is, we get to
continue to grow it.
3. No Numbers - No Business
We heard Matt Bacak say that at a seminar when we were
just getting started....
Mannn was he right... in the beginning we tracked income
and expenses. We made sure that we always made more
than the business was spending... or if we spent a little
too much we added another promotion to cover it. This
limited us in ways we didn’t know.
Just off the top of my head - not tracking our business
metrics kept us from:
• 	Hiring employees - I’m talking employees with
salaries of $30-$45k per year - not freelancers.
• 	Advertising our business regularly - we used to just
spend what we had left over.. now each product gets
a separate ad budget which is tracked to know the
ROAS (return on ad spend).
• 	Getting raises and taking vacations... We always
worked - even when we travelled. We may have
lived an “internet lifestyle” but now we’re hitting
a whole new level where we can vacation (and
actually not do any work) and know the business will
Left: Justin and Chaunna on a Disney World vaca-
tion with their two kids.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 42 of 48 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 43
be there when we get back.
How are we tracking our numbers? It’s called a pro-forma.
While it may seem intimidating at first, it’s worth the effort
to put one in place.
I highly recommend hiring a part time CFO, taking a class
on business finances, or hiring a coach who knows how
to teach you about business finances. You’ll be amazed at
how much more you can do with your business once you
really understand how the money works.
4. 192 Checklists?
When I first started my weekly calls with my business
coach we went through all the tasks that Justin and I did
to make money. This included everything from paying
bills, to writing blog posts, to creating products. There
was a lot!
It broke down to about 192 (give or take a few) tasks that
were divided between Justin, myself, and our freelance
programmer. Once I had it all down on paper he said to
me... now write a checklist on how you do every one of
those tasks.
One hundred and ninety-two checklists!!!??! Are you out
of your mind?
I couldn’t believe he actually expected me to do that...
In the end... I did it. I wrote them all. And as our business
grows I write more and more every week. In fact, it’s such
an important element to the success of our business that I
have time blocked on my schedule every Monday to write
new checklists or review and update old ones.
Isn’t this a waste of my time?
Absolutely not. It took me a good two months to really
see the value in checklists. While procrastinating and
whining about having to write them one day, I thought
back to my college days when I was a Pizza Hut Manager.
There was a checklist for opening the store, cleaning the
bathrooms, washing dishes, packing the walk-in fridge,
even pictures for how each pizza should look.
So why are checklists important? Well, they ensure that
the task gets completed as close as possible to how I would
do it. If the checklist is done correctly, it’s like plugging your
brain into a new employee that gets the job without you
needing to be there. Yay more vacations!
In all seriousness, in order to grow our business we had
to treat it as one - which meant burning the midnight oil
and skipping vacations for an entire year! It means tears,
hard work, and sacrifice but after you push through all the
times you wanted to quit - you can stand proud when you
have a sustainable business that grows even when the
economy isn’t and even when you’re not there.
Want to chat more with us? Connect with us on Facebook:
http://facebook.com/TrafficStrategist. DCS
...After you push through all the times you wanted to
quit - you can stand proud when you have a sustainable
business that grows even when the economy isn’t and
even when you’re not there.”
dcsSUCCESS	www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 43 of 48 --

44 | September 2013
By Malcolm Robinson, MalcolmRobinsonsBlog.com
Seriously, how attractive are you? And no I don’t mean
are you easy on the eye, I mean how appealing are you to
your potential leads? Are they drawn to you? Do they look
forward to hearing from you? And most importantly are
they able to relate to you?
In the last two articles I took a closer look at email
marketing, some of the principles of an effective emailing
as well as the nitty gritty of what can make or break your
campaign. Today I “pour on the gravy” so to say. I am going
to delve into one of the ultra powerful dynamics of email
marketing which is to infuse your attractive character into
your campaign.
To start with I need you to understand that when I talk
about your “attractive character” I don’t mean that you
need to have an ultra colorful personality or that you need
to have tight rope walked across the Grand Canyon. Your
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 44 of 48 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 45
attractive character really is “who you are already.” It’s
what attracts people to you on a day to day basis.
Believe it or not there are many people out there who
will be able to relate to you and your story. And this really
is one of the keys in establishing our attraction. It’s the
ability for others to be able to relate to what is happening
in their own lives to your story. What makes it so powerful
is that the moment someone starts to relate to you they
start to develop trust which is key in converting a lead
into a sale, and as we all know that’s the only way you can
make money. Here are a few pointers to ensure you are
infusing your attractive character into your emails.
Always Be Yourself
Don’t try and be someone or something you are not.
This will always, and I mean always come back and bite you
in the rear. People will see through an act and you will end
up having the opposite of the desired effect. Just as in the
real world some people like and dislike you - the same will
be true in your online relationships. Just be yourself and
like minded people will naturally be drawn to you.
It’s Like Talking To A Friend
It’s important that you converse with your leads in the
same way you would speak with friends. This will put
them at ease and allow the trust factor to develop. When
you speak in this non-threatening way it will allow you to
“pre-sell” without them even realizing what’s going on.
Share Your Strengths And Weaknesses
An incredibly powerful thing happens when you share
both your strengths and weaknesses; it allows you to
appear “normal.” Chances are your leads will share many
of your strengths and weaknesses which will allow them
to relate to you.
Reveal Your Successes And Failures
Be sure to share both your successes and your failures with
your subscribers. This is so very important because when
you share your success it allows your lead to feel that it
is possible to achieve success. And when you share your
failures it allows them to relate to their own failure. You are,
to a large degree, who they wish to become so knowing
that you failed before you succeeded allows them to once
again build on the trust you are developing.
Tell Your Story (And If You Don’t Have
One Then That’s Your Story)
• 	Story telling is how we share our character.
• 	Story telling is how our subscribers relate to us.
• 	Story telling is what makes us
real.
dcs-X	www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 45 of 48 --

46 | September 2013
• 	Story telling is how we effect emotion in people.
• 	Story telling gets the message across in a non-
threatening way.
For years the most important messages in life have been
handed down from generation to generation in the form of
stories. Its powerful, so be sure to tell your story and always
remember that this is relationship marketing.
Below is an example of an email in one of my campaigns
where I get a message across in the form of a story and
where I infuse some of my attractive character. DCS
SUBJECT: THE ELEPHANT
IN THE ROOM...
I could feel my pupils dilating and my pulse
speeding up as a little smile started to creep
onto my face..
I was starting to imagine how I was going to
spend all this money..
New Car?
New House?
Holiday with my family?
Maybe I would start my own charity.
I was sitting in front of my computer and
had just discovered the world of Internet
marketing.
It started with typing three simple words into
Google, three words that would dramatically
impact my life......
"Make money online.."
I was an IM newbie and was about to lose my
Internet virginity.
My innocence was about to be taken away
from me.
I was staring at a sales page for an online
make money opportunity that was going to
make me more money than I would be able to
spend, and I believed every word.
Whatever.............
The truth is I was about to embark on 12
months of pain, failure, misery, and losing
massive amounts of money along the way.
What I didn't know at the time is that this
is the same fate that awaits 97% of Internet
marketers.....
You see this is the elephant in the room, its the
one thing no-one wants to talk about. In fact
some just plain lie about it.
If it was so easy surely everybody would be
doing it but they are not.
And the reason they aren't doing it is because
its isn't easy... and when you are starting out
the odds are massively stacked against most
people.
The funny thing that 90% of the people don’t
know is that if they only followed the core
principles life would be so much easier and
more profitable.
(Check out page 17 and 18 to see why most
people are destined to fail before they even
start:)
<< Link to URL Offer >>
(I tell you this much, If I had some of this
knowledge when I started out I would have
made a lot less mistakes and saved a ton of
time and money.)
But I didn't, so I spent a year in a place I'm sure
many of you can relate to. (PURE FRUSRATION)
Fortunately I am incredibly stubborn and
refused to give up, and because I persisted it
finally paid off.
10 months ago I came across a teaching that
changed everything for me.
It was an understanding of a few basic
principles,
Principles that would get me to understand the
nature of the Internet, and more importantly
how I was going to be able to leverage it in
order to create income for myself.
It’s so simple its scary............
Want to know what they are?
I'm sure you do :-) LOL
Tomorrow I am going to show you a video I
made which lays out exactly how the affiliate
process works.
It’s a powerful training and you will see some
of the core principles which will create a
foundation for you to start to build a massive
business of your own.....
See you tomorrow
Malcolm
"telling it as it is"
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com
